2604.07036.title: ReDAct: Uncertainty-Aware Deferral for LLM Agents
2604.07036.paper_summary: Recently, LLM-based agents have become increasingly popular across many applications, including complex sequential decision-making problems. However, they inherit the tendency of LLMs to hallucinate, leading to incorrect decisions. In sequential settings, even a single mistake can irreversibly degrade the trajectory, making hallucinations an even bigger problem. Although larger LLMs hallucinate less, they incur a significantly higher per-token cost. In this paper, we address this tradeoff by proposing ReDAct (Reason-Defer-Act). In ReDAct, an agent is equipped with two LLMs: a small, cheap model used by default, and a large, more reliable but expensive model. When the predictive uncertainty of the small model exceeds a calibrated threshold, the decision is deferred to the large model. We evaluate our approach in text-based embodied environments such as ALFWorld and MiniGrid and show that deferring only about 15% of decisions to the large model can match the quality of using it exclusively, while significantly reducing inference costs.
2604.07036.paper_summary_zh: 最近，基於LLM的代理在許多應用中變得越來越受歡迎，包括複雜的序列決策問題。<lb/>然而，它們繼承了LLM的幻覺傾向，導致錯誤的決策。<lb/>在序列環境中，即使是一個錯誤也可能不可逆轉地降低軌跡，使得幻覺成為一個更大的問題。<lb/>雖然較大的LLM幻覺較少，但它們的每個標記成本顯著更高。<lb/>在本文中，我們通過提出ReDAct（Reason-Defer-Act）來解決這一權衡。<lb/>在ReDAct中，代理配備了兩個LLM：一個小型、便宜的模型作為默認使用，和一個大型、可靠但昂貴的模型。<lb/>當小型模型的預測不確定性超過校準閾值時，決策將推遲到大型模型。<lb/>我們在基於文本的具身環境中評估我們的方法，例如ALFWorld和MiniGrid，並顯示將約15%的決策推遲給大型模型可以匹配專門使用它的質量，同時顯著降低推理成本。
2604.07036.author: Dzianis Piatrashyn et.al.
2604.07036.authors: Dzianis Piatrashyn, Nikita Kotelevskii, Kirill Grishchenkov, Nikita Glazkov, Ivan Nasonov, Ilya Makarov, Timothy Baldwin, Preslav Nakov, Roman Vashurin, Maxim Panov
